methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Rainmaker
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Dronamics
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Intramotev
private.rationale: Same sector (Drones & Autonomous), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Natilus is a Series A autonomous systems company in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The target has raised $16M to date. The broader sector includes 8 private peers (Zipline, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, Elroy Air among them) and no public comparables. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage companies with comparable metrics to support a reliable peer-median valuation.

Stage-progression analysis was the applicable method here. Using total raised ($16M) as a base proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, the method implies a valuation of $32M. This reflects observed progression patterns as companies transition from Series A to later rounds, anchoring to the capital deployed rather than operating metrics or revenue given the company's stage.

The triangulation range is $27M–$37M, centered at $32M median. This single-method result carries material uncertainty: the peer-median approach could not be reliably applied due to insufficient comparable-stage datapoints in the Drones & Autonomous universe, leaving only stage-progression to anchor the valuation. The range assumes typical step-up dynamics hold for this company. Key unknowns include near-term revenue trajectory, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and market timing for next funding round. Additional diligence on unit economics and runway would refine the estimate.
